# Clause 4 — Conformance (ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018)

> Source: **ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018** *Systems and software engineering — Life cycle processes — Requirements engineering*, Clause 4 *Conformance* (pp. 8–9).
> Cleaned, structure-preserving extract for the `iso-29148-software-requirements` skill.

## 4.1 Intended usage

This document provides guidance for the execution of the ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 and ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207 processes that deal with requirements engineering. It also provides normative definition of the content and recommendations for the format of the information items (or documentation) that result from the implementation of these processes.

**Users of this document can claim conformance to the process provisions or to the information item provisions, or both.**

## 4.2 Full conformance

A claim of full conformance is equivalent to claiming conformance:

- to the provisions contained in **5.2.4** (Requirements construct), **5.2.5** (Characteristics of individual requirements), **5.2.6** (Characteristics of a set of requirements), and **5.2.7** (Requirement language criteria);
- to the requirements-engineering-related processes of **ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288** and **ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207** cited in **Clause 6.1**;
- to the **information items specified in Clause 7** (BRS, StRS, SyRS, SRS); and
- to the **required content of the information items in Clause 9 and Annex A**.

## 4.3 Conformance to processes

The standard provides requirements for a number of requirements engineering processes suitable for usage during the life cycle of a system, product or service. The process requirements are contained in **Clause 6.1**.

## 4.4 Conformance to information item content

The standard provides requirements for a number of requirements engineering information items that are to be produced during the life cycle. A claim of conformance to the information item provisions means that:

- the **required information items** stated in the standard **are produced**; and
- it is demonstrated that the information items produced during the requirements engineering activities **conform to the content requirements** defined in the standard.

The requirements for information items are in **Clause 7**; the content requirements are in **Clause 9 and Annex A**.

> **NOTE 1** — If a user claims full conformance to ISO/IEC/IEEE 15289, it does *not* imply conformance to the information items / content of this document, because 29148 adds additional information items.
>
> **NOTE 2** — For simplicity of reference, each information item is described as if it were a separate published document. However, information items **can be considered conforming** if they are:
>
> - unpublished but available in a repository for reference;
> - divided into separate documents or volumes; or
> - combined with other information items into one document.
>
> The information items **do not require physical documentation**, so long as the required content is easily available and logically organised (e.g., Model-Driven Development approaches keep almost all system information in a modelling tool — that is acceptable).

## 4.5 Tailored conformance

### 4.5.1 Processes

The standard does not provide for tailoring life-cycle processes. Tailoring of system / software life-cycle processes is provided in **ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2015 Annex A** and **ISO/IEC/IEEE 12207:2017 Annex A** respectively.

### 4.5.2 Information items

When this document is used as a basis for establishing a set of information items that **do not qualify for full conformance**, the clauses shall be selected or modified in accordance with the tailoring process prescribed in **Annex C**.

- The **tailored text**, for which tailored conformance is claimed, **must be declared**.
- Tailored conformance is achieved by demonstrating that the requirements for the information items, as tailored, have been satisfied — using the outcomes of the tailoring process as evidence.

## Summary — what a conformance statement must declare

A conformance statement at the head of a specification should explicitly state:

| Element | Example wording |
|---|---|
| Which conformance posture | "This document claims **full conformance** to ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018." |
| or, for partial | "This document claims conformance to the **information item provisions** of ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018 (Clauses 7, 9, Annex A); process conformance is **not** claimed." |
| or, for tailored | "This document claims **tailored conformance** to ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148:2018. Tailored clauses are listed in Appendix X; rationale is provided per Annex C." |
| Which life-cycle process basis | "Implements requirements engineering activities of ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288:2015 (system) / 12207:2017 (software)." |
